study_types: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)
Electron microscopy - micrographs or particle stacks

items.title: European Nucleotide Archive (ENA)
items.description: The European Nucleotide Archive (ENA) provides a comprehensive record of the world's nucleotide sequencing information, covering raw sequencing data, sequence assembly information, and functional annotation.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: ArrayExpress
items.description: ArrayExpress is a public repository for functional genomics data, providing access to high-throughput functional genomics datasets, including microarray and RNA-seq data, along with associated metadata.

items.title: European Genome-phenome Archive (EGA)
items.description: The European Genome-phenome Archive (EGA) is a secure repository for storing and sharing personally identifiable genetic and phenotypic data, providing controlled access to sensitive datasets for research purposes.

items.title: Federated European Genome-phenome Archive (FEGA) Sweden
items.description: The Federated European Genome-phenome Archive (FEGA) is an infrastructure for transnational discovery and access to sensitive human research data.FEGA Sweden is a national repository for genomic data, providing secure storage and controlled access to genomic datasets generated in Sweden, with a focus on supporting research and collaboration within the country.

items.title: European Variation Archive (EVA)
items.description: The European Variation Archive (EVA) is a public repository for genetic variation data, providing access to a wide range of genetic variants, including single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs), insertions and deletions (indels), and structural variants, along with associated metadata.

items.title: GWAS Catalog
items.description: The GWAS Catalog is a public repository for genome-wide association study (GWAS) data, providing access to a comprehensive collection of GWAS results, including associations between genetic variants and traits or diseases, along with associated metadata.

items.title: Proteomics Identifications Database (PRIDE)
items.description: PRIDE is a public data repository for proteomics data, providing access to mass spectrometry-based proteomics datasets, including raw data, processed results, and associated metadata.

items.title: wwPDB OneDep
items.description: wwPDB OneDep is a unified system for the deposition, validation, and biocuration of macromolecular structures, providing access to high-quality structural data.

items.title: Protein Data Bank in Europe (PDBe)
items.description: PDBe is a public repository for macromolecular structure data, providing access to high-quality structural data, including atomic coordinates, experimental data, and associated metadata.

items.title: Electron Microscopy Data Bank (EMDB)
items.description: The EMDB is a public repository for 3D electron microscopy density maps, providing access to high-resolution structural data.

items.title: MetaboLights
items.description: MetaboLights is a repository for metabolomics data, providing access to experimental data, metadata, and protocols related to metabolomics studies.

items.title: BioImage Archive (BIA)
items.description: The BioImage Archive (BIA) is a public repository for biological imaging data, providing access to a wide range of imaging datasets, including microscopy images, associated metadata, and protocols.

items.title: EMPIAR
items.description: EMPIAR is a public repository for raw electron microscopy image data, providing access to high-resolution electron microscopy datasets, including raw images, metadata, and associated protocols.
study_type: Electron microscopy - micrographs or particle stacks

items.title: BioSamples
items.description: BioSamples is a repository for biological sample information, providing access to metadata about biological samples used in various experiments. By linking to BioStudies, BioSamples allows researchers to access comprehensive metadata about the samples used in a study, facilitating data integration and reuse across different datasets and studies.

items.title: BioStudies
items.description: BioStudies is a repository for biological study information, providing access to metadata about biological studies, including experimental design, protocols, and associated datasets. By linking to BioSamples, BioStudies allows researchers to access comprehensive metadata about the samples used in a study, facilitating data integration and reuse across different datasets and studies.

items.title: MINSEQE (Minimum Information about a high-throughput SEQuencing Experiment)
items.description: MINSEQE is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a high-throughput sequencing experiment, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, sequencing platform, and data processing.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: MIAME (Minimum Information About a Microarray Experiment)
items.description: MIAME is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a microarray experiment, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, array design, and data processing.

items.title: ENA Sample Checklists
items.description: The ENA Sample Checklists are metadata standards that define the required and recommended metadata fields for biological samples submitted to the European Nucleotide Archive (ENA), ensuring consistent and comprehensive sample metadata for genomic data submissions.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: MIMS (Minimum Information about a Metagenome Sequence)
items.description: MIMS is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a metagenome sequence, including details about the organism, sequencing platform, assembly method, and associated metadata.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: REMBI (Recommended Metadata for Biological Images)
items.description: REMBI is a metadata standard that defines the recommended metadata fields for biological imaging data, providing guidelines for the consistent and comprehensive annotation of imaging datasets, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, imaging modality, and data processing.

items.title: MITI (Minimum Information about a Tissue Imaging Experiment)
items.description: MITI is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a tissue imaging experiment, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, imaging modality, and data processing.

items.title: MIAPE (Minimum Information About a Proteomics Experiment)
items.description: MIAPE is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a proteomics experiment, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, mass spectrometry analysis, and data processing.

items.title: CIMR (Chemical Information for Metabolomics Reporting)
items.description: CIMR is a metadata standard that defines the chemical information required for reporting metabolomics data, including details about the chemical entities, their properties, and associated metadata.

items.title: MSI (Metabolomics Standards Initiative)
items.description: The Metabolomics Standards Initiative (MSI) is a metadata standard that defines the minimum information required to describe a metabolomics experiment, including details about the experimental design, sample preparation, analytical techniques, and data processing.

items.title: .fasta (FASTA format)
items.description: FASTA is a text-based file format for representing nucleotide sequences or peptide sequences, where each sequence is represented by a header line starting with '>' followed by the sequence data.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: .fastq (FASTQ format)
items.description: FASTQ is a text-based file format for storing both nucleotide sequences and their corresponding quality scores, commonly used for high-throughput sequencing data.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: .gff (General Feature Format)
items.description: GFF is a file format used for describing genes and other features of DNA, RNA, and protein sequences, providing a standardized way to represent genomic annotations.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: .embl (EMBL format)
items.description: EMBL is a file format used for storing nucleotide sequences and their associated annotations, providing a standardized way to represent genomic data in the European Molecular Biology Laboratory (EMBL) database.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: .bam (Binary Alignment/Map format)
items.description: BAM is a binary file format for storing aligned sequence data, providing a compact and efficient way to represent large sequencing datasets, including information about the alignment of reads to a reference genome.
study_type: Analysis of an environmental community (e.g. sea water, human gut)

items.title: .vcf (Variant Call Format)
items.description: VCF is a standardized file format for storing gene sequence variations, including information about the position of the variant, the reference and alternate alleles, and associated metadata.

items.title: .bed (Browser Extensible Data format)
items.description: BED is a file format used for storing genomic regions and annotations, providing a simple and flexible way to represent features such as genes, exons, and regulatory elements on a reference genome.

items.title: mzTab
items.description: mzTab is a standardized file format for mass spectrometry-based proteomics and metabolomics data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of identification and quantification results.

items.title: mzIdentML
items.description: mzIdentML is a standardized file format for mass spectrometry-based proteomics data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of peptide and protein identification results.

items.title: mzML
items.description: mzML is a standardized file format for mass spectrometry data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of proteomics and metabolomics data.

items.title: nmrML
items.description: nmrML is a standardized file format for nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) spectroscopy data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of NMR data.

items.title: mzQuantML
items.description: mzQuantML is a standardized file format for mass spectrometry-based proteomics data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of quantitative proteomics results.

items.title: OME-TIFF
items.description: OME-TIFF is a standardized file format for storing and sharing biological imaging data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of microscopy images and associated metadata.

items.title: OME-Zarr
items.description: OME-Zarr is a standardized file format for storing and sharing large-scale biological imaging data, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of microscopy images and associated metadata in a cloud-friendly format.

items.title: ISA-Tab
items.description: ISA-Tab is a standardized file format for representing experimental metadata in a tab-delimited format, designed to facilitate the exchange and analysis of metadata for multiomics studies.
